perturbation.original_text: Except as agreed upon by mutual consent, the Joint Venturers shall not be required to make any capital contribution to the Joint Venture.
perturbation.changed_text: The Joint Venturers shall be required to make capital contributions to the Joint Venture, with amounts to be determined solely by Collectible Concepts Group, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction regarding capital contributions. Originally, contributions were optional ('Except as agreed upon by mutual consent'). Now, contributions are mandatory ('shall be required'), and the amount is unilaterally determined by one party (Collectible Concepts Group, Inc.), conflicting with the original mutual consent clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Joint Venturers shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Joint Venturers recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses. It is agreed that either Joint Venturer shall, except as provided for below, have authority to execute instruments of any character relating to the affairs of the Joint Venture; provided, that without the written consent or approval of both of the Joint Venturers: (i) the Joint Venture shall incur no liability of any sort, nor any indebtedness for borrowed funds; (ii) no assets owned in the name of the Joint Venture be disposed of; and (iii) no commitment to purchase any item for the Joint Venture shall be made. 39
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that capital contributions were optional, requiring mutual consent. The changed text makes capital contributions mandatory and gives Collectible Concepts Group, Inc. sole authority to determine the amounts. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Joint Venturers have full charge of all affairs and requires written consent from both parties for incurring liabilities or indebtedness. By unilaterally mandating contributions, Collectible Concepts Group, Inc. circumvents the joint decision-making process outlined in the contradicted text and imposes a potential liability without mutual agreement, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Joint Venture shall commence on the 1st of March, 2003, and shall be effective until February 28, 2004 unless extended by written agreement of the Joint Venturers not less than thirty (30) days prior to scheduled termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The Joint Venture shall commence on the 1st of March, 2003, and shall be effective indefinitely, with termination at the sole discretion of Pivotal Self Service Tech, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction regarding the term of the joint venture. Initially, it was for a fixed term ('until February 28, 2004') with a requirement for mutual agreement to extend. Now, it is 'effective indefinitely' and can be terminated unilaterally by Pivotal Self Service Tech, Inc.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding on the Joint Venturers and their respective heirs, successors, executors, administrators, and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a fixed term for the joint venture, requiring mutual agreement for extension. The changed text makes the joint venture indefinite and terminable at the sole discretion of one party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the agreement is binding on the joint venturers and their successors. If the agreement can be terminated at any time by one party, it is not truly binding on successors, executors, etc., as the other party has no recourse to continue the agreement. This constitutes a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction as it violates the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Distributions may only be made if after any distribution is made, the Joint Venture assets are in excess of all liabilities of the Joint Venture. Each distribution shall be made ratably to the Joint Venturers according to their prorata interest in the Joint Venture as shown in Section 6.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributions will be made at the discretion of Collectible Concepts Group, Inc. with no requirement for assets to exceed liabilities and no consideration given to pro rata interests.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that distributions can be made only if assets exceed liabilities and they must be ratable. The modified text says distributions are at the discretion of one party without those requirements. This creates in-text contradiction in enforcement because the change gives complete distribution control to one party, violating the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Division of Income and Losses. All income and credits, and all losses and deductions shall be owned and shared among the Joint Venturers as follows: 50% to Collectible Concepts Group, Inc. 50% to Pivotal Self Service Tech, Inc. Depreciation and all other charges and expenses, which are not expressly apportioned by this Agreement, shall be apportioned in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, consistently applied.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text specify a pro rata division of income, losses, and distributions. The changed text gives complete discretion to Collectible Concepts Group, Inc. for distributions, explicitly negating the pro rata requirement and the asset exceeding liability requirement. This creates a direct contradiction, as distributions may no longer align with the 50/50 division of income and losses outlined in the 'Division of Income and Losses' section.
